q: The pyrimidine bases are cytosine and (A. adenine B. guanine C. thymine D. purine). (2026)
The two strands of a (A. RNA B. sugar C. DNA D. nucleotide) molecule are not identical but are complementary to each other. (2025)
Adenine and thymine are joined by (A. carbon B. hydrogen C. nitrogen D. phosphate) bonds. (2025)
DNA is a double helix structure consisting of (A. two, B. three, C. four, D. many) polynucleotide strands. (2024)
(A. Hydrogen, B. Covalent, C. Ionic, D. Polar) bonds link each complementary base pair in the structure of DNA. (2024)
Base pairing leads to DNA being able to replicate and to transfer information to (A. rRNA B. codon C. tRNA D. mRNA). (2025)
Single-stranded binding (A. enzymes B. DNA C. proteins D. RNA) act as scaffolding, holding the two strands apart. (2026)
(A. Helicase B. Topoisomerase C. Primase D. DNA ligase) enzyme unwinds parental double helix at replication forks. (2025)
(A. Topoisomerase B. DNA ligase C. Helicase D. DNA polymerase) enzyme unwind the double helix and unzip the two parental strands. (2025)
DNA polymerase replicates the (A. two B. three C. four D. many) original strands differently. (2025)
DNA replication is the making of an exact copy of the (A. sugar, B. nucleotide, C. RNA, D. DNA) molecule. (2024)
In transcription (A. mRNA B. IRNA C. rRNA D. codon) is synthesized based on the template of a gene. (2026, 2025)
In transcription, mRNA is synthesized based on the (A. RNA B. tRNA C. rRNA D. DNA) template of a gene. (2025)
The central dogma of molecular biology stated that genetic information flows from DNA to (A. Protein B. Amino acid C. Molecule D. Nucleotide). (2025)
The two steps in protein synthesis are transcription and (A. initiation, B. elongation, C. translation, D. termination). (2024, 2025)
Transcription occurs in the (A. cytoplasm, B. ribosome, C. nucleus, D. vacuole) of the cell. (2026, 2024, 2025)
The second stage of protein synthesis is (A. translation B. elongation C. initiation D. description). (2025)
Each (A. mRNA, B. RNA, C. tRNA, D. codon) carries the specific amino acid at a time at its acceptor stem. (2025)
Vitamin A deficiency can cause (A. night blindness B. heart disease C. blindness D. lung disease). (2025)
One type of antibody made by (A. wheat B. paddy C. barley D. com) can deliver radioisotopes to tumour cells. (2026, 2025)
The orange pigment beta-carotene which can be converted into (A. vitamin A, B. vitamin B, C. vitamin C, D. vitamin D). (2024)
